title: Object Stamp object groups
description:
## Slice Summary
Extend Object Stamp to Object Groups as top-level Design Objects. When the user samples a group, stamping places a new group with cloned member objects, fresh identities, preserved group membership, and anchored placement.

## User Value
Designers can repeat composite arrangements as reusable spatial units while keeping each stamped arrangement independently editable.

## Out of Scope
Do not support arbitrary multi-selection unless it is already an Object Group. Do not copy planning entries, Species Catalog records, or non-canvas Design state.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Extend Object Stamp to Object Groups as top-level Design Objects.

**Current behavior:**
After the earlier slices, Object Stamp supports Placed Plants, Zones, and Annotations as single top-level Design Objects. Existing grouped members resolve to their Object Group during top-level interactions, but Object Stamp does not yet clone groups as grouped composites.

**Desired behavior:**
Object Stamp can sample one visible and unlocked top-level Object Group and repeatedly place anchored group copies. Sampling a grouped member should load the Object Group, not the individual member. Each stamp creates cloned member objects with fresh identities, creates a new Object Group preserving membership among the cloned members, translates the cloned arrangement so the sampled anchor lands at the clicked point, selects the new Object Group, and creates one undoable Scene Edit. The clone must remain a real group, independently editable from the source group. Placement must respect the same snapping, ghost preview, cancellation, and lock/visibility rules as earlier Object Stamp slices.

**Key interfaces:**
- Top-level hit testing for grouped members and Object Groups.
- Scene-owned cloning for grouped Placed Plants, Zones, Annotations, and Object Group membership.
- Scene Edit history, selection, lock/visibility, and Object Stamp preview/cancellation state.

**Out of scope:**
- Stamping arbitrary multi-selection that has not been converted into an Object Group.
- Adding nested group editing beyond preserving supported existing group relationships.
- Copying planning entries or non-canvas Design state.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Object Stamp can sample a visible, unlocked Object Group by clicking the group or one of its grouped members.
- [ ] Stamping a sampled Object Group creates fresh cloned member objects and a fresh Object Group that references only those cloned members.
- [ ] The cloned group preserves the source arrangement relative to the sampled anchor and places that anchor at the snapped click location.
- [ ] The new Object Group is selected after placement, remains independently editable, and the source group is unchanged.
- [ ] Each group stamp creates one undoable Scene Edit; undo removes the stamped group and its cloned members together.
- [ ] Ghost preview, snapping, Escape cancellation, tool-change clearing, and design-replacement clearing work for Object Group sources.
- [ ] Hidden or locked Object Groups cannot be sampled, and placement is blocked if the relevant source layer is no longer visible or unlocked.
- [ ] Regression tests cover mixed-member group cloning, member identity remapping, group selection, history, and lock/visibility blocking.
close_reason: Implemented Object Stamp support for Object Groups with grouped-member sampling, cloned member identities, remapped group membership, anchored placement, preview, selection, undoable history, and lock/layer guards. Verified with focused scene interaction/runtime tests and TypeScript check.

title: Object Stamp zones and annotations
description:
## Slice Summary
Extend Object Stamp from the Placed Plant tracer to existing Zones and Annotations. The user can sample one visible and unlocked top-level Zone or Annotation, see a loaded preview, and place anchored copies repeatedly.

## User Value
Designers can reuse authored spatial structure such as beds, areas, and text notes without copy/paste offset behavior.

## Out of Scope
Do not stamp Object Groups or multi-selection in this slice. Do not create or copy planning entries.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Extend Object Stamp to top-level Zones and Annotations.

**Current behavior:**
After the tracer slice, Object Stamp can sample and stamp one existing Placed Plant. Zones and Annotations still rely on normal selection, drawing, or copy/paste behavior.

**Desired behavior:**
Object Stamp can sample one visible and unlocked top-level Zone or Annotation and repeatedly place anchored copies. The sampled click point remains the placement anchor, ghost preview reflects the loaded object, snapping applies to the anchor, and each placed copy is selected after placement while Object Stamp remains active and loaded. Zone clones must get collision-safe names or identities as appropriate, preserve their geometry relative to the anchor, and continue to behave as real Zones. Annotation clones must get fresh identities, preserve authored text and presentation fields, and continue to behave as real Annotations. Locked or hidden layers/objects cannot be sampled, and stamping should be blocked if the source layer becomes hidden or locked before placement.

**Key interfaces:**
- Top-level Design Object hit testing and layer lock/visibility rules.
- Scene-owned Zone and Annotation cloning, identity generation, anchored translation, selection, and history.
- Object Stamp ghost preview and cancellation state shared with the Placed Plant tracer.

**Out of scope:**
- Stamping Object Groups or arbitrary multi-selection.
- Adding new Zone or Annotation editing capabilities.
- Copying timeline actions, budget items, consortium entries, or other non-canvas Design state.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Object Stamp can sample one visible, unlocked top-level Zone and place anchored Zone copies repeatedly.
- [ ] Object Stamp can sample one visible, unlocked top-level Annotation and place anchored Annotation copies repeatedly.
- [ ] Zone clones preserve source geometry relative to the sampled anchor and receive collision-safe identities or names.
- [ ] Annotation clones preserve authored text and presentation fields while receiving fresh identities.
- [ ] Each stamped Zone or Annotation placement creates one undoable Scene Edit and selects the new copy.
- [ ] Ghost preview, snapping, Escape cancellation, tool-change clearing, and design-replacement clearing work for Zone and Annotation sources.
- [ ] Hidden or locked Zones and Annotations cannot be sampled, and placement is blocked if the relevant source layer is no longer visible or unlocked.
- [ ] Regression tests cover Zone and Annotation sampling, preview/anchor behavior, identity generation, selection, history, and lock/visibility blocking.
close_reason: Implemented Object Stamp support for top-level zones and annotations with anchored cloning, preview, snapping, collision-safe zone names, fresh annotation ids, selection/history behavior, and lock/visibility guards. Verified with focused scene interaction tests and TypeScript check.

title: Object Stamp placed-plant tracer
description:
## Slice Summary
Add a thin, end-to-end Object Stamp workflow for one existing Placed Plant. The user can choose Object Stamp from the left canvas toolbar, click a visible and unlocked Placed Plant to sample it, then click elsewhere on the canvas to place anchored copies until they change tools or press Escape.

## User Value
Designers can quickly repeat an existing Placed Plant with its design-specific presentation instead of returning to the Species Catalog or using offset copy/paste.

## Out of Scope
Do not stamp Zones, Annotations, Object Groups, multi-selection, planning entries, timeline actions, budget items, or consortium entries in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add the first end-to-end Object Stamp workflow for sampling and repeatedly placing one existing Placed Plant.

**Current behavior:**
Canopi has a Plant Stamp workflow that starts from Species Catalog data and places new Placed Plants. Existing canvas copy/paste can duplicate selected Design Objects, but paste uses an offset and is not an anchored repeat-placement tool. The left canvas toolbar does not expose an Object Stamp tool.

**Desired behavior:**
The left canvas toolbar exposes Object Stamp as a separate tool from Plant Stamp. In Object Stamp mode, the first click on a visible and unlocked Placed Plant samples that Placed Plant and records the exact clicked world point as the placement anchor. After sampling, moving over the canvas shows a ghost preview of the loaded copy. Each subsequent canvas click places a cloned Placed Plant so that the sampled anchor lands at the clicked point, applying the same snapping behavior as other placement tools. Each stamped copy is selected after placement, Object Stamp stays active and loaded, and each placement creates one undoable Scene Edit. Pressing Escape, changing tools, or replacing the design clears the loaded source and returns to/selects the normal tool state. Object Stamp must not mutate normal copy/paste clipboard state.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas command surface and active tool switching.
- Scene interaction hit testing, snapping, keyboard cancellation, hover/preview, and Scene Edit history.
- Scene-owned Placed Plant cloning and selection behavior.
- Toolbar commands, shortcuts if assigned, tooltip text, and i18n.

**Out of scope:**
- Stamping Zones, Annotations, Object Groups, or multi-selection.
- Copying timeline actions, budget items, consortium entries, Species Catalog records, or other non-canvas Design state.
- Replacing or renaming the existing Plant Stamp workflow.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Object Stamp appears as a distinct left-toolbar tool with localized label and tooltip text.
- [ ] Selecting Object Stamp and clicking a visible, unlocked Placed Plant loads that Placed Plant without dirtying the Design or adding undo history.
- [ ] After sampling, a ghost preview follows pointer movement and uses the sampled click point as the placement anchor.
- [ ] Clicking the canvas after sampling creates a new Placed Plant clone at the anchored snapped location.
- [ ] Each stamped Placed Plant gets a fresh identity, preserves relevant source Placed Plant fields, and is selected after placement.
- [ ] Object Stamp remains active and loaded after each placement until the user changes tools, presses Escape, or the design is replaced.
- [ ] Each stamped placement creates one undoable Scene Edit; undo removes one stamped copy at a time.
- [ ] Hidden or locked Placed Plants cannot be sampled, and stamping is blocked if the source layer is no longer visible or unlocked.
- [ ] Object Stamp does not read from or overwrite the normal copy/paste clipboard state.
- [ ] Focused regression tests cover sampling, anchored placement, snapping, history, selection feedback, cancellation, and clipboard isolation.
close_reason: Implemented Object Stamp placed-plant tracer with toolbar/command/i18n support, anchored sampling and placement, snapping, cancellation, lock/visibility guards, undo history, clipboard isolation, and focused frontend tests plus TypeScript check.

title: Keep Elliptical Zone radii stable when dragging
description:
Dragging an Elliptical Zone with the select tool currently changes the ellipse shape because the drag path translates the stored radii point as if it were a vertex. The drag should move the ellipse center while preserving radii.

## Required Skills
diagnose
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Selecting and dragging an Elliptical Zone moves its center by the drag delta.
- [ ] The Elliptical Zone radii remain unchanged after dragging.
- [ ] Regression coverage exercises the interaction drag path through SceneInteractionController.
- [ ] Focused frontend tests and TypeScript check pass.
close_reason: Fixed Elliptical Zone dragging by preserving the radii point during select-tool drag; only the center translates. Root cause: ellipse geometry stores points as center plus radii, but the shared Zone drag path translated every point as if it were polygon vertices. Tests: cd desktop/web && npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit; cd desktop/web && npm test.

title: Add live Elliptical Zone Measurements
description:
## Slice Summary
Extend Zone Measurements to Elliptical Zones during drawing and for a single selected Elliptical Zone.

## User Value
Designers can see width, height, and area while drawing or inspecting an Elliptical Zone.

## Out of Scope
Do not add rotated ellipse support, collision avoidance, or persistent measurement annotations in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Extend Zone Measurements to Elliptical Zones.

**Current behavior:**
Zone Measurement overlay support is planned for rectangular Zones, and Elliptical Zone creation is tracked separately. Elliptical Zones have no Zone Edges, so edge-label behavior does not apply directly.

**Desired behavior:**
While drawing an Elliptical Zone, the measurement overlay shows live width, height, and area for the snapped bounding box that will be committed. When exactly one top-level Elliptical Zone is selected, the overlay shows width, height, and area for that Zone. Measurements remain derived presentation only and do not create annotations, history entries, or persisted document data.

**Key interfaces:**
- Shared Zone Measurement helpers and renderer-independent overlay
- Elliptical Zone geometry using center plus radii
- Single selected top-level Zone presentation
- Metric distance and area formatting

**Out of scope:**
- Zone Edge labels for Elliptical Zones
- Rotated ellipse measurements
- Collision avoidance with plants, annotations, or other labels
- Configurable imperial/custom units
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] During Elliptical Zone drawing, width, height, and area labels update live from the snapped preview geometry.
- [ ] When exactly one top-level Elliptical Zone is selected, width, height, and area labels are shown.
- [ ] Elliptical Zone measurements are suppressed for multi-selection and group selection.
- [ ] Measurement values use compact metric distance and area formatting.
- [ ] Measurements are not persisted into `.canopi` data and do not create annotations or history entries by themselves.
- [ ] Focused frontend tests cover draft updates, selected Elliptical Zone measurements, suppression, formatting, and non-persistence behavior.
- [ ] Required frontend checks are run, including `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` and focused Vitest coverage.
close_reason: Implemented live Elliptical Zone Measurements for drawing and single selected Elliptical Zones, including width, height, area, multi-selection suppression, non-persistence coverage, and compact metric formatting via shared helpers. Tests: cd desktop/web && npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts zone-measurements.test.ts; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Add drag-to-create Elliptical Zones
description:
## Slice Summary
Add an ellipse tool that creates Elliptical Zones by dragging a bounding box and committing one Zone through the canvas scene edit path.

## User Value
Designers can define rounded or oval planting areas instead of being limited to rectangular and polygonal Zones.

## Out of Scope
Do not add post-creation resize handles, rotation, or persistent measurement annotations in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add drag-to-create Elliptical Zone creation.

**Current behavior:**
The scene runtime already understands Elliptical Zone geometry in renderer, hit testing, and bounds paths, but the canvas does not expose a tool that lets designers create Elliptical Zones.

**Desired behavior:**
The ellipse tool lets a designer drag a bounding box to create an Elliptical Zone. The snapped drag start and snapped current pointer define opposite corners of the box; the committed Zone derives its center and radii from that normalized box. Pointer movement previews the ellipse while drawing, and pointer-up commits one Scene Edit when the ellipse is valid. The created Zone is selected after commit.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas toolbar, command registry, and keyboard shortcut definitions
- SceneInteractionController pointer handling for drawing tools
- Existing Elliptical Zone persisted geometry using center plus radii
- Renderer, hit testing, and camera bounds behavior for Elliptical Zones

**Out of scope:**
- Rotated ellipses
- Post-creation resize handles
- Zone Measurement labels
- Persistent measurement annotations
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The canvas exposes an ellipse tool through the existing toolbar and command/tool selection paths.
- [ ] Dragging with the ellipse tool previews an Elliptical Zone inscribed in the snapped drag bounding box.
- [ ] Pointer-up commits one valid Elliptical Zone Scene Edit using center plus radii geometry and selects the created Zone.
- [ ] Dragging in any direction normalizes to positive radii and the correct center.
- [ ] Too-small or degenerate ellipses are not committed.
- [ ] Existing renderer, hit testing, camera bounds, and serialization behavior for Elliptical Zones still agree with the center-plus-radii model.
- [ ] Focused frontend tests cover toolbar/command exposure, creation, normalization, snapping, invalid commit suppression, and selection.
- [ ] Required frontend checks are run, including `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` and focused Vitest coverage.
close_reason: Implemented drag-to-create Elliptical Zones via toolbar, command, shortcut, preview, snapped center/radii commit, selection, invalid suppression, and renderer/bounds/serialization coverage. Tests: cd desktop/web && npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts commands-registry.test.ts shortcuts-manager.test.ts canvas-toolbar-plant-color.test.tsx camera-controller.test.ts pixi-scene.test.ts; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Add Zone Measurement overlay foundation
description:
## Slice Summary
Add a renderer-independent canvas overlay for derived Zone Measurements and use it for rectangular Zones while drawing and when one top-level Zone is selected.

## User Value
Designers can see Zone edge lengths and area directly in the canvas without creating saved annotations or stale duplicated data.

## Out of Scope
Do not add polygonal Zone creation in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add a renderer-independent overlay for derived Zone Measurements.

**Current behavior:**
Designers can draw and select rectangular Zones, but the canvas does not show derived edge lengths or area for the Zone.

**Desired behavior:**
The canvas shows derived Zone Measurements as a screen-space overlay while drawing a Zone and when exactly one top-level Zone is selected. Rectangular Zones show horizontal edge-length labels near readable edge midpoints plus one area label. Labels are presentation only; they are not saved as annotations or document data. Measurement values describe world-space geometry and remain stable across zoom, while placement and short-edge hiding use viewport scale.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas runtime interaction and selection surfaces
- Renderer-independent HTML overlay above Pixi/Canvas2D renderers
- Shared Zone Measurement geometry and formatting helpers
- Scene query/presentation paths for single selected top-level Zones

**Out of scope:**
- Polygonal Zone creation
- Persistent measurement annotations
- Collision avoidance with plants, annotations, or other labels
- Configurable non-metric units
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] While drawing a rectangular Zone, the canvas shows live edge lengths and area based on the geometry that will be committed.
- [ ] When exactly one top-level rectangular Zone is selected, the canvas shows edge lengths and area for that Zone.
- [ ] Measurements are suppressed for multi-selection and group selection.
- [ ] Edge labels stay horizontal, sit near edge midpoints, and hide when the screen-space edge is too short to read.
- [ ] Area labels remain visible for valid Zone areas.
- [ ] Measurements are not persisted into `.canopi` data and do not create annotations or history entries by themselves.
- [ ] Focused frontend tests cover drawing, selection, suppression, formatting, and non-persistence behavior.
- [ ] Required frontend checks are run, including `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` and focused Vitest coverage.
close_reason: Implemented renderer-independent Zone Measurement overlay foundation for rectangular Zones while drawing and when a single top-level Zone is selected, with suppression/readability/formatting coverage. Tests: cd desktop/web && npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts scene-runtime.test.ts zone-measurements.test.ts; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Add click-to-place polygonal Zone creation
description:
## Slice Summary
Add a polygon tool that creates polygonal Zones by placing vertices, previewing the active edge, and committing one Zone when closed.

## User Value
Designers can define non-rectangular Zones with precise vertex placement instead of being limited to rectangles.

## Out of Scope
Do not add post-creation vertex editing or plant pattern filling in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add click-to-place polygonal Zone creation.

**Current behavior:**
The canvas has a rectangular Zone drawing path, but designers cannot create polygonal Zones by placing vertices.

**Desired behavior:**
The polygon tool lets a designer click to place vertices for a transient polygonal Zone draft. Pointer movement previews the active edge from the last committed vertex to the current snapped pointer position. The draft is not persisted until the polygon is closed. Closing a valid polygon creates one Zone through the Scene Edit path and selects the created Zone. Escape cancels the draft, Backspace removes the last vertex, and invalid or degenerate polygons are not committed.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas toolbar and command/tool selection
- SceneInteractionController live pointer and keyboard behavior
- Scene Edit transaction boundaries for creating canvas-owned Zones
- Zone serialization through existing scene persistence

**Out of scope:**
- Post-creation vertex editing
- Persistent measurement annotations
- Plant pattern fill or spacing tools
- Polygon boolean operations or self-intersection repair
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The canvas exposes a polygon tool through the existing tool selection path.
- [ ] A click starts a transient polygonal Zone draft, and subsequent clicks add vertices.
- [ ] Pointer movement previews the active edge using snapped geometry when snapping is enabled.
- [ ] A valid polygon can be closed by an intentional close action such as clicking the first vertex, double-clicking, or pressing Enter.
- [ ] Closing creates one polygonal Zone scene edit and selects the created Zone.
- [ ] Escape cancels the draft without dirtying or persisting a Zone.
- [ ] Backspace removes the last draft vertex without affecting document state outside the draft.
- [ ] Invalid or degenerate polygons are not committed.
- [ ] Focused frontend tests cover start, add vertex, preview, close, cancel, undo-like vertex removal, and invalid commit behavior.
- [ ] Required frontend checks are run, including `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` and focused Vitest coverage.
close_reason: Implemented click-to-place Polygonal Zone creation with toolbar, command, shortcut, live active-edge draft overlay, Enter and first-vertex close actions, Escape cancel, Backspace vertex removal, degenerate suppression, snapped preview geometry, selection, and serialization coverage. Tests: cd desktop/web && npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts commands-registry.test.ts shortcuts-manager.test.ts canvas-toolbar-plant-color.test.tsx; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Make Zone drawing snap-aware
description:
## Slice Summary
Make transient Zone drawing previews and committed Zone geometry honor enabled snap-to-grid and snap-to-guides settings.

## User Value
When a designer draws a Zone, the preview, measurements, and saved geometry describe the same snapped result.

## Out of Scope
Do not add polygonal Zone creation or measurement labels in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Zone drawing previews and committed geometry should honor active snapping settings.

**Current behavior:**
Some Zone drawing paths can commit raw pointer geometry even when snap-to-grid or snap-to-guides is enabled. This makes future live measurements risk describing a different geometry than the one users expect from the snapping controls.

**Desired behavior:**
While a Zone is being drawn, the preview geometry and the committed Zone geometry use the same snap-adjusted points when snapping is enabled. Snap behavior should remain disabled when the relevant settings are off. The change should preserve existing selection, undo/redo, dirty-state, and render invalidation behavior.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas tool interaction for drawing Zones
- Snap-to-grid and snap-to-guides settings
- Scene Edit transaction boundaries for canvas-owned state
- Public runtime behavior covered by interaction tests

**Out of scope:**
- Polygonal Zone creation
- Zone Measurement labels
- Post-creation vertex editing or resizing
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Zone drawing previews use snap-adjusted geometry when snap-to-grid or snap-to-guides is enabled.
- [ ] Committed Zone geometry matches the previewed snapped geometry.
- [ ] Existing unsnapped Zone drawing behavior remains unchanged when snapping is disabled.
- [ ] Focused frontend tests cover snapped and unsnapped drawing behavior through the runtime interaction surface.
- [ ] Required frontend checks are run, including `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` and focused Vitest coverage.
close_reason: Implemented snap-aware rectangular Zone drawing preview and commit geometry for grid and guide snapping. Tests: cd desktop/web && npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Add live polygonal Zone Measurements
description:

## Out of Scope

design:

**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Extend Zone Measurements to polygonal Zones during drawing and selection.

When exactly one top-level polygonal Zone is selected, the overlay shows each readable Zone edge length and the Zone area. Measurements remain derived presentation only and respect Zone layer visibility.

**Key interfaces:**

**Out of scope:**

- Vertex editing after Zone creation
- Configurable imperial/custom units
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] During polygonal Zone drawing, committed edge labels update as vertices are added.
- [ ] The active edge label updates smoothly as the pointer moves.
- [ ] When the draft preview can form a valid polygon, the closing edge and live area are shown.
- [ ] When exactly one top-level polygonal Zone is selected, readable edge labels and area are shown.
- [ ] Measurements are suppressed for multi-selection and group selection.
- [ ] Edge labels stay horizontal, sit near edge midpoints, and hide when the screen-space edge is too short to read.

close_reason: Harmonized PanelBar with CanvasToolbar icon-button behavior: shared custom tooltip, locale-reactive labels, ARIA-state active styling, focused regression test, and frontend typecheck passed.

title: Local Problem Report Flow
description:
## Slice Summary
Add the first user-visible Problem Report path. A user can create a local report package from the app and receives an easy-to-find folder containing a Report Summary and Diagnostic Bundle.

## User Value
A non-technical user has concrete files they can share through any future support channel without needing to find app logs manually.

## Out of Scope
Automatic upload, email integration, sensitive attachments, and folder reveal/open behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add the first local Problem Report flow that creates easy-to-find report artifacts.

**Current behavior:**
Canopi writes backend logs and surfaces some frontend errors, but users do not have a guided way to create a Problem Report or collect troubleshooting evidence.

**Desired behavior:**
A user can start a Problem Report from the app, provide a short description, and create a timestamped local folder that is easy to locate. The folder contains a readable Report Summary and a Diagnostic Bundle with privacy-safe support evidence by default. The success state shows the folder path and supports copying the Report Summary text.

**Key interfaces:**
- Problem Report creation command or workflow.
- Diagnostic Bundle contents contract.
- Report Summary text contract.
- App menu or command registry entry for the user entry point.
- Settings, subsystem health, backend logs, and frontend-provided context as support evidence sources.

**Out of scope:**
- Automatic upload or send behavior.
- Email draft integration.
- Opening or revealing the folder in the operating system file manager.
- Opt-in sensitive attachments such as current Design contents, screenshots, or precise Location.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A user-visible command starts a Problem Report flow without requiring developer tools.
- [ ] Creating a report writes a timestamped folder with `Report Summary.txt` and `Diagnostic Bundle.zip`.
- [ ] The generated folder is easy for a non-technical user to locate, with Desktop as the preferred default when available.
- [ ] The Report Summary includes the user's description plus safe app, platform, and report metadata.
- [ ] The Diagnostic Bundle includes privacy-safe support evidence by default and excludes Design contents, precise Location, screenshots, and unsanitized filesystem paths.
- [ ] The success state shows the exact folder path and provides a Copy Summary action.
- [ ] Focused tests cover bundle creation, default privacy exclusions, and the user-facing report flow.
close_reason: Implemented local Problem Report flow with typed IPC, privacy-safe Diagnostic Bundle creation, Help menu/dialog UI, i18n coverage, and focused backend/frontend tests. Verified with cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::problem_report::tests::creates_report_summary_and_diagnostic_bundle_in_visible_folder, npm test -- problem-report-dialog.test.tsx commands-registry.test.ts i18n-completeness.test.ts, npx tsc --noEmit, cargo fmt --all -- --check, and npm run check:types.

title: Frontend Diagnostics Capture
description:
## Slice Summary
Capture frontend runtime errors so future Diagnostic Bundles include recent UI-side failures in addition to backend logs.

## User Value
Problem Reports become useful for UI failures that currently only appear in the developer console.

## Out of Scope
Remote telemetry, analytics, user behavior tracking, and screenshot capture.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Capture recent frontend errors for inclusion in Diagnostic Bundles.

**Current behavior:**
Frontend failures are mostly written to the browser console, so a user's Diagnostic Bundle would miss UI-side errors unless a developer is present.

**Desired behavior:**
The app keeps a bounded, privacy-conscious in-memory record of recent frontend errors and handled diagnostic events. Error boundaries and command failures feed that record. Diagnostic Bundle creation can include those recent frontend diagnostics without tracking user behavior or uploading data.

**Key interfaces:**
- Frontend diagnostics collector.
- Error boundary reporting.
- Command and workflow error reporting seams.
- Diagnostic Bundle frontend evidence input.

**Out of scope:**
- Remote telemetry, analytics, session replay, or usage tracking.
- Capturing screenshots or Design contents.
- Persisting an indefinite frontend log history.
- Replacing backend logging.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Recent frontend errors are captured in a bounded diagnostic buffer.
- [ ] ErrorBoundary failures are recorded in the diagnostic buffer.
- [ ] Command or workflow failures that are already logged to the console can be routed into the diagnostic buffer.
- [ ] Diagnostic entries avoid sensitive Design data and avoid full unsanitized filesystem paths.
- [ ] Diagnostic Bundle creation can include the captured frontend diagnostics.
- [ ] Focused tests cover buffer bounds, recorded error shape, and Diagnostic Bundle inclusion.
close_reason: Implemented bounded frontend diagnostics for Problem Reports, routed ErrorBoundary and command failures into the buffer, included sanitized frontend-diagnostics.json in Diagnostic Bundles, and verified with focused Vitest/Rust tests plus tsc and check:types.

title: Show Problem Report Folder

## Out of Scope

design:

**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Let users reveal the generated Problem Report folder from the app.

- Problem Report success state.
- Folder path returned by Problem Report creation.

**Out of scope:**

acceptance_criteria:

title: Fix Species Catalog token relevance review findings
description:
Review found that query-side Common Name tokenization diverges from the prepare-db token index for punctuation and Unicode case folding, and that explicit non-relevance sorts still pay token join cost even though relevance ordering is unused.

## Required Skills
tdd,craft

title: Implement snappy broad-query behavior
description:
## Slice Summary
Implement the approved short-query and count policy for broad Species Catalog searches while preserving committed-result lifecycle, pagination, and visible search feedback.

## User Value
Short or broad searches no longer block the Species Catalog Workbench, while designers still get useful results and feedback.

## Out of Scope
Do not change relevance tiers beyond what the approved policy requires. Do not rewrite unrelated Workbench state.
design:
## Agent Brief

**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Implement the approved short-query and broad-query policy so Species Catalog search remains snappy.

**Current behavior:** Broad searches can spend too much time collecting candidates and counts, which makes the Species Catalog Workbench feel slow even when the first useful page could be shown quickly.

**Desired behavior:** The Workbench applies the approved short-query policy and keeps interaction responsive for broad searches. First-page results should appear quickly, count behavior should match the decision bead, and stale committed results should remain stable while a new broad search is in flight.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog search request/result contract
- Species Catalog Workbench loading and pagination behavior
- Species Catalog latency harness

**Out of scope:** Do not change relevance ranking except where necessary to obey the approved broad-query policy.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The implementation follows the policy recorded by the short-query decision bead.
- [ ] Broad-query first-page latency satisfies the agreed harness budget.
- [ ] Result count behavior matches the decision bead for broad searches.
- [ ] Previously committed results remain visible or stable according to the approved Workbench behavior while a new search is pending.
- [ ] Pagination, structured filters, and explicit non-name sorts still work for broad searches.
close_reason: Implemented Species Catalog broad-query policy in the Workbench search session: one-character normalized text clears locally without backend search, active text searches omit exact first-page counts, committed rows remain visible while pending, and pagination still uses next_cursor. Tests: cd desktop/web && npm test -- src/__tests__/plant-search-session.test.ts; cd desktop/web && npm test -- src/__tests__/plant-db-controller.test.ts; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit; cd desktop/web && npm test.

title: Rank current-locale Common Name whole-token matches first
description:
## Slice Summary
Make active-locale whole-token Common Name matches rank ahead of Canonical Name and longer-token prefix matches in Species Catalog text search.

## User Value
A designer searching French `lin` sees `Lin commun`, `Lin bisannuel`, and related French Common Names before `Lindleya mespiloides`.

## Out of Scope
Do not add multi-word phrase ranking beyond what is necessary for whole-token behavior. Do not change broad-query count policy.
design:
## Agent Brief

**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Rank active-locale Common Name whole-token matches ahead of Canonical Name prefixes and long-token matches.

**Current behavior:** A query such as French `lin` can rank `Lindleya mespiloides` near the top because prefix matching treats long taxonomy/name tokens as competitive with whole Common Name tokens. Runtime Common Name probes have been explored but are too slow for interactive search.

**Desired behavior:** Active-locale Common Name whole-token matches are ranked first using indexed or precomputed name data. For French `lin`, plants with `Lin ...` Common Names should appear ahead of `Lindleya mespiloides`, while explicit non-name sorts and structured filters keep their existing behavior.

**Key interfaces:**
- Generated Species Catalog search-name index or equivalent indexed data
- Species Catalog search locale
- Relevance ordering used by the Species Catalog Workbench

**Out of scope:** Do not implement phrase/all-token ranking, broad-query policy changes, or sort UI copy changes in this slice.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Active-locale Common Name whole-token matches rank ahead of Canonical Name prefix matches for relevance-sorted searches.
- [ ] French `lin` ranks active-locale `Lin ...` Common Names before `Lindleya mespiloides`.
- [ ] The implementation avoids per-row correlated Common Name scans at query time.
- [ ] First-page latency for representative short Common Name queries stays within the harness budget.
- [ ] Structured filters and explicit non-name sorts keep existing behavior.
- [ ] Agent docs are updated if Species Catalog schema or search-index rules change.
close_reason: Implemented active-locale Common Name whole-token relevance ranking with a generated indexed species_search_common_name_tokens table, schema v9 generator support, compatibility fallback for older DBs, focused relevance/planner tests, database docs, and verification via py_compile, prepare-db to /tmp, v9 latency harness, cargo fmt check, cargo check, clippy, and cargo test --workspace.

title: Add phrase and all-token Common Name relevance tiers
description:
## Slice Summary
Extend Species Catalog relevance so multi-word Common Name searches prefer exact phrase matches, then all-token active-locale Common Name matches, before broader prefix matches.

## User Value
A designer searching `lin commun` gets the expected exact Common Name match first, while partial multi-word searches remain intuitive.

## Out of Scope
Do not revisit the short-query/count policy. Do not alter Species Catalog Filter behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief

**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add phrase and all-token relevance tiers for multi-word active-locale Common Name searches.

**Current behavior:** After whole-token matching exists, multi-word Common Name searches can still be ordered mostly by broad prefix/token behavior. Exact phrase matches and all-token matches are not represented as clear ranking tiers.

**Desired behavior:** Active-locale Common Name exact phrase matches rank first for multi-word queries, followed by active-locale Common Names containing all query tokens, followed by broader partial matches. This should generalize across locales through the same normalization/search-name data used by the whole-token slice.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog query normalization
- Generated Species Catalog search-name index or equivalent indexed data
- Relevance ordering used by the Species Catalog Workbench

**Out of scope:** Do not alter short-query policy, fallback-language tiering, or explicit non-name sort behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A search for French `lin commun` ranks the exact active-locale Common Name phrase first.
- [ ] Active-locale Common Names containing all query tokens rank ahead of broader prefix-only or single-token matches.
- [ ] Multi-word queries continue to support pagination and structured filters.
- [ ] Normalization behavior, including diacritics when already supported by Species Catalog search, is covered by focused tests.
- [ ] Explicit non-name sorts are unaffected.
close_reason: Implemented multi-word active-locale Common Name relevance tiers: exact phrase before all-token matches before broader BM25/taxonomy matches, with diacritic-normalized query tokens, focused fixture and planner tests, docs update, v9 latency harness run, cargo fmt check, cargo check, clippy, and cargo test --workspace.

title: Decide Species Catalog Workbench sort presentation for best match
description:
## Slice Summary
Choose how the Species Catalog Workbench should present effective best-match ranking while the user enters text: keep implicit relevance under Name, show Best match, remove explicit Relevance, or another product choice.

## User Value
The visible sort control accurately communicates why text search results are ordered by relevance.

## Out of Scope
Do not implement the UI change in this decision bead. Do not change backend ranking.
design:
## Agent Brief

**Category:** decision
**Summary:** Decide how the Species Catalog Workbench sort UI should present best-match relevance during text search.

**Current behavior:** The Workbench can effectively rank by relevance during text search while visible sort controls may suggest a different or overly specific sort, such as name order. That can make the result ordering feel inconsistent.

**Desired behavior:** A human decision records the user-facing sort semantics for empty text, active text search, explicit non-name sorts, and whether a visible Best Match/Relevance option should exist. Downstream implementation should know exactly what the control displays and how it maps to backend sort requests.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog Workbench SearchBar and SortSelect behavior
- Species Catalog search sort request
- UI copy and localization keys

**Out of scope:** Do not implement UI or backend changes in this decision bead.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The decision records the visible sort label and default behavior when no search text is active.
- [ ] The decision records the visible sort label and default behavior when text search is active.
- [ ] The decision records how explicit non-name sorts interact with text search relevance.
- [ ] The decision records whether a visible Best Match/Relevance option exists and what copy it uses.
- [ ] The decision gives enough detail for an agent to update UI behavior and localization without asking a follow-up product question.
notes: Decision: The Species Catalog Workbench should present text-search ranking as Best match, not Relevance. Empty text remains browse mode: the visible default sort is Name and the backend request uses Sort::Name unless the user has selected another explicit sort. When active text has at least two normalized characters and the user has not explicitly chosen a sort for that text search, the visible sort is Best match and the backend request uses Sort::Relevance. Best match should be a visible option using the copy key currently represented by plantDb.sortRelevance, but the English label should be Best match and all locales should be updated with equivalent user-facing copy rather than the technical word Relevance. Explicit sorts remain available during text search. If the user selects Family, Height, Hardiness, Growth rate, or Name while text is active, that selected sort overrides Best match and the backend request sends the selected Sort value. Clearing text returns the automatic default to Name; implementation may keep a separate auto/default state so a text-triggered Best match default does not permanently overwrite the user's browse sort. One-character text follows the broad-query policy and should not show Best match results because no backend text search is issued.
close_reason: Recorded sort-presentation decision: empty browse defaults to Name, active text defaults to visible Best match backed by Sort::Relevance, explicit user-selected sorts override best-match ranking, and localization should use user-facing Best match copy. Decision-only bead; tests not run.

title: Decide short-query and count behavior for broad Species Catalog searches
description:
## Slice Summary
Choose the product and performance policy for one-character and very broad Species Catalog text searches, including whether exact total counts must be returned immediately.

## User Value
The team can make search feel snappy without surprising designers or hiding too much feedback for broad searches.

## Out of Scope
Do not implement the chosen policy in this decision bead. Do not change ranking tiers here.
design:
## Agent Brief

**Category:** decision
**Summary:** Decide product policy for very short and broad Species Catalog searches before implementing snappy broad-query behavior.

**Current behavior:** One-character and broad queries can generate many Species Catalog candidates. The expected behavior for minimum query length, exact count timing, stale result retention, and user-visible loading/copy is not written down as a durable product decision.

**Desired behavior:** A human decision records how very short and broad searches should behave in the Species Catalog Workbench, including whether counts are exact/immediate, delayed, capped, or omitted. Downstream implementation should not need to reopen the product question.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog Workbench search input and count display
- Species Catalog search request/result contract
- Pagination and loading-state behavior

**Out of scope:** Do not implement backend or UI changes in this decision bead.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The decision records the intended behavior for one-character queries.
- [ ] The decision records whether first-page result counts must be exact and immediate for broad queries.
- [ ] The decision records any user-visible loading, stale-result, or count-copy behavior needed in the Species Catalog Workbench.
- [ ] The decision states enough constraints for an agent to implement broad-query behavior without asking a follow-up product question.
notes:
Decision: Species Catalog Workbench text search should treat normalized one-character queries as too broad and should not issue a backend text-search request for them. The Workbench should clear the current text-search results for that one-character intent, hide the count, and use the existing no-results state rather than adding new instructional copy. Empty text remains normal browse mode and should still load the first page with an exact count.

For active text searches with at least two normalized characters, first-page responsiveness is more important than an immediate exact total. The first-page request should be allowed to omit exact total counting for text searches; when total count is omitted or unknown, SearchBar should hide the count chip. Pagination remains driven by next_cursor, not total_estimate. Structured-filter-only browsing and empty-text browsing may continue to request exact counts immediately.

While a debounced or in-flight text search is pending, previously committed results should remain visible until the new request commits or resolves to the one-character empty state. Loading state may still be exposed through existing busy indicators. Downstream implementation should preserve explicit non-name sort behavior and should not change relevance tiers in this policy slice.
close_reason: Recorded broad-query policy: one-character text searches are too broad and should not hit backend search; active text first-page requests may omit exact counts and hide the count chip while preserving cursor pagination and committed-result stability. No code tests required for decision-only bead.

title: Demote fallback-language and taxonomy matches below active-locale names
description:
## Slice Summary
Make Species Catalog relevance explicitly prefer active-locale Common Names, then fallback-language Common Names, then Canonical Name, family, and genus matches.

## User Value
Search results match user expectations in non-English locales without hiding useful Canonical Name or taxonomy matches.

## Out of Scope
Do not remove Canonical Name search. Do not change display-name fallback behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief

**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Demote fallback-language and taxonomy matches below active-locale Common Name matches in relevance ordering.

**Current behavior:** Common Names from fallback languages and taxonomy fields can intermix too early with active-locale Common Name matches. That makes localized search feel less intuitive when users search in their current UI language.

**Desired behavior:** Relevance uses explicit tiers: active-locale Common Name matches first, fallback-language Common Name matches next, then Canonical Name/family/genus matches, then broad long-token/prefix matches. Canonical Name search should still work; it should just stop outranking strong active-locale Common Name hits.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog search locale and fallback-name data
- Canonical Name, family, and genus text matching
- Relevance ordering used by the Species Catalog Workbench

**Out of scope:** Do not change which names are displayed in results or alter Common Name fallback display rules.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Active-locale Common Name matches rank ahead of fallback-language Common Name matches for relevance-sorted searches.
- [ ] Fallback-language Common Name matches remain discoverable when the active locale has no useful match.
- [ ] Canonical Name, genus, and family searches still return relevant plants.
- [ ] Tests cover at least one non-English locale where fallback/taxonomy matches previously competed with active-locale names.
- [ ] Result display fallback behavior is unchanged.
close_reason: Implemented explicit relevance tiers for active-locale Common Name matches, fallback English Common Name matches, then BM25/taxonomy matches. Tests: cargo test -p canopi-desktop db::query_builder; cargo test -p canopi-desktop db::plant_db::search; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace.

title: Add Species Catalog search relevance fixtures and latency harness
description:
## Slice Summary
Create durable Species Catalog search relevance fixtures and a lightweight latency harness that exercises representative active-locale Common Name searches and broad short queries.

## User Value
Maintainers can verify that relevance work actually improves cases such as French `lin`, `lin commun`, and `lind` while keeping first-page search responsive.

## Out of Scope
Do not change ranking behavior in this slice. Do not redesign the Species Catalog Workbench UI.
design:
## Agent Brief

**Category:** task
**Summary:** Add representative Species Catalog search relevance fixtures and a repeatable latency harness before changing ranking.

**Current behavior:** Species Catalog search has limited durable coverage for relevance cases such as French `lin`, multi-token Common Names, and broad short queries. Relevance and latency can regress without a focused signal.

**Desired behavior:** Maintainers have fixtures and a harness that exercise active-locale Common Names, multi-token Common Names, fallback/taxonomy behavior, and broad queries. The harness should separate first-page list latency from total-count latency so future ranking changes can be judged without guesswork.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog search request/result contract
- Generated Species Catalog database and search index
- Species Catalog Workbench search behavior

**Out of scope:** Do not change ranking behavior, search UI behavior, or generated database schema in this slice unless needed only to make the harness runnable.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Fixtures cover French `lin`, `lin commun`, `lind`/`lindleya`, and at least one English Common Name case.
- [ ] The harness reports first-page list latency and total-count latency separately for representative queries.
- [ ] The harness runs safely when the bundled Species Catalog database exists and fails or skips with a clear message when it is unavailable.
- [ ] Existing search behavior is unchanged except for added tests or measurement utilities.
close_reason: Added Species Catalog search relevance fixtures for French lin/lin commun/lind and English apple, added an ignored manual latency harness reporting list and count timings separately, documented the harness command, and verified with focused search tests, manual harness run, cargo fmt check, cargo check, clippy, and cargo test --workspace.

title: Align Species Catalog Workbench sort UI with effective ranking
description:
## Slice Summary

## User Value

## Out of Scope
Do not change backend relevance ranking. Do not redesign unrelated Species Catalog Workbench controls.
design:
## Agent Brief

**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Align Species Catalog Workbench sort controls with the effective backend ranking policy.

**Current behavior:** Visible sort state can diverge from the effective search ranking, especially when text search implicitly uses best-match relevance. This makes it harder for users to understand why results appear in their current order.

**Desired behavior:** The Workbench sort UI implements the approved presentation policy. The visible control state, localized copy, and backend sort request should agree for empty search, active text search, and explicit non-name sorts.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog Workbench SearchBar and SortSelect components

- UI localization files

**Out of scope:** Do not change backend relevance ranking in this UI alignment slice.

title: Deepen Species Catalog search planner seams
description:
## Slice Summary
Simplify the Species Catalog backend search planner by separating text normalization, relevance tiers, structured filters, projection, and pagination into clearer seams.

## User Value

## Out of Scope

design:
## Agent Brief

**Category:** task
**Summary:** Refactor Species Catalog search planner seams so relevance rules can evolve without fragile SQL assembly.

**Current behavior:** Search planning responsibilities are tightly coupled: query normalization, relevance ranking, filters, projection, pagination, and count/list predicate sharing are difficult to reason about independently.

**Desired behavior:** The search planner has clear internal seams for text normalization, relevance tiers, structured filters, projection, pagination, and count/list predicate reuse. The refactor should preserve behavior from the completed ranking slices while making future relevance changes safer and easier to test.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog search request/result contract
- Species Catalog Filter predicate construction
- Relevance ordering and pagination
- Count/list query consistency

**Out of scope:** Do not introduce new user-visible ranking behavior in this refactor.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Search planner code separates normalization, relevance-tier construction, structured filters, projection, pagination, and count/list predicate reuse into clear units.
- [ ] Existing relevance and filter tests continue to pass without weakening assertions.
- [ ] Count and list queries continue to share equivalent filter semantics.
- [ ] The refactor does not introduce new user-visible ranking behavior.
- [ ] Agent docs are updated if database/search planner ownership or commands change.
close_reason: Deepened Species Catalog search planner seams without changing ranking behavior: text normalization, relevance tiers/token joins, shared predicates, pagination, and projection now live in separate query_builder modules while SpeciesSearchPlan remains the public interface. title: Deepen Species Catalog Filter and Design Session modules
description:
## Slice Summary
Track the two approved architecture improvements from the architecture review: deepen Species Catalog Filter behavior and deepen Design Session lifecycle behavior.

## User Value
The codebase gains locality and leverage around two high-friction seams without broad rewrites or contract churn.

## Out of Scope
Do not pursue Target planning projection, canvas runtime surface changes, Site Adaptation refactors, broad folder renames, shared SpeciesFilter contract replacement, or autosave rewrites unless a child bead proves they block the tracer bullets.
design:
## Design Brief
Track the approved architecture improvement as a narrow, resumable epic.

The epic contains two tracer bullets:
- Deepen Species Catalog Filter behavior with `life_cycle`.
- Deepen Design Session lifecycle behavior by unifying attached and detached transition selection.

Architectural decisions already made:
- Keep `SpeciesFilter` plus `extra` stable as the external shared request contract for now.
- Normalize Species Catalog Filter behavior internally before considering a shared contract replacement.
- Keep generated filter metadata declarative; adapters own executable behavior.
- Use `life_cycle` as the first Species Catalog Filter tracer because it proves bespoke predicate behavior.
- Use **Design Session** as the domain term for the active Design runtime context.
- Let Design Session choose attached versus detached canvas behavior internally.
- Leave autosave out of the first Design Session slice except to preserve current behavior.

Discarded candidates from the review:
- Target planning projection.
- Canvas runtime surface changes.
- Site Adaptation rules.

Do not revive discarded candidates unless a child bead proves they are blocking a tracer bullet.

title: Unify Design Session transition attached and detached paths
description:
## Slice Summary
Use Design transition selection as a tracer bullet for a deeper Design Session lifecycle module: callers request a transition and the module decides whether a canvas runtime is attached.

## User Value
Open, new, template, queued, and mount-existing Design flows become harder to call out of order because dirty guard, normalization, dirty baselines, workflow install, canvas chrome, and zoom sequence live behind one Design Session seam.

## Out of Scope
Do not rename document-session folders, rewrite autosave, change .canopi contracts, move all save composition, or touch Target planning, MapLibre, or canvas runtime surfaces unless a test proves they block this transition seam.
design:
## Design Brief
Deepen Design Session lifecycle behavior using attached/detached transition selection as the tracer bullet.

Callers should request a Design transition. The Design Session implementation should decide whether a canvas runtime is attached and apply the correct attached or detached path internally. Dirty guard, save-before-replace, normalization, state replacement, dirty baseline reset, workflow install, canvas chrome, and zoom ordering should become harder to call out of order.

Use this slice to reduce the current caller-visible split between `transitionDocument()` and `transitionDetachedDocument()`. Keep autosave out of the first slice except where existing call sites must keep working.

## Relevant Files
- `CONTEXT.md`
- `docs/agent/document-lifecycle.md`

title: Deepen Species Catalog Filter behavior with life cycle tracer
description:
## Slice Summary
Use the life_cycle Species Catalog Filter as a tracer bullet for a deeper filter behavior module while keeping the external SpeciesFilter plus extra request contract stable.

## User Value
Adding or changing Species Catalog Filters becomes safer because UI behavior, active filter formatting, request normalization, option loading, and Rust predicate building stop leaking fixed-filter rules across callers.

## Out of Scope
Do not replace the shared SpeciesFilter contract, migrate every filter in one pass, change user-visible filter behavior, add dependencies, or rework Species Catalog search pagination.
design:
## Design Brief
Deepen Species Catalog Filter behavior using `life_cycle` as the tracer bullet.

The external shared request contract remains `SpeciesFilter` plus `extra`; this bead normalizes fixed and dynamic filter behavior behind internal frontend and Rust seams. Generated filter metadata should stay declarative. Executable behavior belongs in adapters that interpret metadata.

Use `life_cycle` because it is user-facing filter behavior backed by bespoke Rust predicates over `is_annual`, `is_biennial`, and `is_perennial`, not a single SQL column. The slice should prove that UI placement is separate from predicate behavior and that a Species Catalog Filter domain key is separate from any SQL column implementation.

Preserve current user-visible behavior. Keep the change narrow enough that follow-up beads can migrate `sun_tolerances`, `soil_tolerances`, `climate_zones`, `edibility_min`, and `nitrogen_fixer` after the seam proves itself.
